topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to vibrate under my gaze. A C-minus. It was a catastrophic deviation from the A-level performance I had meticulously crafted for this history paper. My hands, resting on the desk, began to tremble, a fine, almost imperceptible shaking that I fought to suppress by pressing my fingernails into my palms. I reread the feedback, the professor’s terse notes seeming to mock my effort. A slow, cold knot tightened in my stomach, spreading outward like spilled ink. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor, and walked to the window. Outside, the afternoon sun felt harsh and unforgiving, mirroring the sharp, unwelcome clarity of my current situation.

[story 2]

"A D?" The sound of the word felt alien in my ears, hollow and sharp. I stared at the grade displayed on the screen, the numbers swimming before my eyes. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as if my lungs had momentarily forgotten how to function correctly. I clenched my jaw so tightly that a dull ache bloomed behind my teeth. I started pacing the small confines of my room, the worn rug muffling the frantic rhythm of my footsteps. Every shadow in the corner seemed to deepen, and I kept glancing at the door, half-expecting someone to materialize and demand an explanation for this unexpected failure. My throat felt thick, constricted, making it hard to form a coherent sound.

[story 3]

I picked up the graded assignment, the paper feeling impossibly heavy in my hands. The score was a stark, undeniable verdict. My vision tunneled slightly; the edges of the room seemed to blur into an indistinct wash of muted color. I didn't move immediately. Instead, I sat down heavily on the edge of my bed, the mattress sighing under the sudden weight. A low, ragged exhale escaped my lips, a sound more like a deflating balloon than a breath. I began to trace the pattern on the wooden surface of my desk with my index finger, the repetitive motion an attempt to anchor myself against the swirling uncertainty that had taken hold of my entire composure.
